candidates.name: Greg Gianforte
candidates.notes: 25th Governor of Montana (2021-). Tech entrepreneur. Strong evangelical Christian identity — perhaps first Montana governor from Fundamentalist tradition. NRA A rating. Signed constitutional carry, abortion restrictions, ban on gender-affirming care for minors. Young Earth Creationist donor.

profile.education: Stevens Institute of Technology (BE & MS, Electrical Engineering)
profile.background: Tech entrepreneur who co-founded RightNow Technologies; U.S. Representative (2017-2021); became 25th governor in 2021.

candidates.name: Steve Daines
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from Montana (2015-). Chair of NRSC. Heritage Action lifetime 79%. NRA A+ — highest possible rating. Presbyterian. Fifth-generation Montanan. Strong pro-life record, co-sponsored Pain-Capable Unborn Child Protection Act. Supports border wall and immigration enforcement.

profile.background: Former Procter & Gamble and RightNow Technologies executive. Lived in Asia for several years on business. Served one term in U.S. House before winning Senate seat in 2014.
profile.office: 320 Hart Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 609 S. Central Ave., Suite #4, Central Plaza Building

aipac-track.title: TrackAIPAC — Montana delegation
aipac-track.excerpt: Aggregates every AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby contribution each member of Congress has received, sourced directly from FEC filings and OpenSecrets. Matches the RESOLUTE Citizen America-First question 3: foreign-backed lobby donations.

aipac-opensecrets.excerpt: Non-partisan campaign-finance database. Search the candidate's name to see every FEC-reported donor, including pro-Israel lobby contributions.

aipac-endorsements.title: AIPAC Election Center — endorsed candidates
aipac-endorsements.excerpt: AIPAC's own public endorsement list. If a candidate appears here, they have accepted AIPAC backing; the RESOLUTE Citizen scoring criterion treats this as a failure on America-First question 3.

candidates.name: Tim Sheehy
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from Montana (2025-). Former Navy SEAL. Catholic — attends St. John Vianney Parish in Belgrade, MT. NRA endorsed with $2M ad buy. Heritage Action not yet scored as new member. Youngest Republican senator. Defeated Jon Tester (D) in 2024. Pro-life, pro-2A, strong border security positions.

profile.background: Former Navy SEAL officer with multiple combat deployments. Founded Bridger Aerospace aerial firefighting company and Ascent Vision Technologies. Youngest Republican senator.
profile.office: 124 Russell Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 2900 4th Ave N, Suite 201, William J. Jameson Federal Building
125 West Granite St, Suite 200, Silver Bow Center
